session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9114 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [195 lines omitted] ...
131→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 133→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 171→ "error": null 185→## Error Case 199→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 217→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by following the steps. First, I'll fetch the PR feedback and detect the trunk branch:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Detect the trunk branch for diff context

{ "success": true, "pr_number": 9114, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9114", "pr_title": "Fix: `erk pr list` and `erk dash` exclude learn plans by default to match TUI behavior", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5zKBv_", "path": "src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py", "line": 130, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Test Coverage**: New feature code added (`--include-learn` flag and filtering logic) without corresponding test file. Erk requires Layer 4 business logic tests for all feature code in `_pr_list_impl` and `_run_interactive_mode` functions.", "created_at": "2026-03-09T23:27:40Z" } ] }, { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5zKC3A", "path": "src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py", "line": 130, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Test Coverage**: New feature (--include-learn flag) added without corresponding test. Erk requires Layer 4 business logic tests for feature code that modifies filtering behavior. Consider adding test cases to tests/commands/pr/test_list.py covering both --include-learn and default exclude-learn behavior.", "created_at": "2026-03-09T23:29:04Z" }

[View workflow run](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22879252651)", "body": "<!-- dignified-python-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-09 16:28:40 PT

No violations detected across 1 Python file. All code changes follow dignified Python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL over EAFP - Compliant
✅ Exception handling - Compliant
✅ Path operations - None affected
✅ Import organization - Compliant
✅ No default parameters - Compliant (Click decorators exempt)
✅ Keyword-only args (5+ params) - All compliant
✅ Frozen dataclasses - Compliant
✅ Subprocess patterns - None modified

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py`: 0 violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-09 16:28:40 PT**: Second review: All functions continue to follow dignified Python standards. New `--include-learn` flag and parameter propagation through call chain are compliant. No violations detected.
- **2026-03-09 16:27:23 PT**: Initial review: No violations detected. All functions properly use keyword-only separators for 5+ parameters. Code follows dignified Python standards.

</details>", "body": "<!-- tripwires-review -->

## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-09 16:28:50 PT

No tripwire violations detected. All code follows erk conventions.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered
- CLI tripwire: "using a non-None default for an optional Click option" → loaded click-framework-conventions.md
- CLI tripwire: "adding or modifying CLI commands without regenerating reference docs" → loaded auto-generated-reference-docs.md

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches (Mechanical)
✅ `subprocess.run` with check=True - None found
✅ `Path.home()` direct usage - None found
✅ `os.chdir()` calls - None found
✅ `time` module imports - None found
✅ `RuntimeError` for CLI failures - None found

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches
✅ **Non-None default for Click option (line 133)** - PASS: `is_flag=True` options have explicit carve-out per click-framework-conventions.md ("Boolean flags are not three-state — they are either present (True) or absent (False)")
✅ **Reference doc regeneration** - PASS: Requirement applies only to `erk exec` commands, not `erk pr` commands (per auto-generated-reference-docs.md)
✅ **Parameter threading** - PASS: `include_learn` properly threaded through all function signatures
✅ **CLI validation** - PASS: Boolean flags rarely need validation per cli-options-validation.md

### Violations Summary
No violations found.

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py`: 0 violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-09 16:28:50 PT**: No violations detected. Triggered 2 CLI tripwires (is_flag default, reference docs), loaded full docs, confirmed both have explicit exceptions that apply to this PR.

</details>",
session.assistant: Now I'll get the file-level restructuring context and analyze the feedback:

Now I'll classify the feedback according to the Comment Classification Model. Let me analyze each thread:
**Analysis:**
1. **Thread 1 & 2** (both at line 130): Test Coverage violations from `github-actions` bot - Classification: **Actionable** (missing test for new feature) - Pre-existing: **false** (new feature code specific to this PR, not a restructuring issue) - Complexity: **cross_cutting** (would require adding tests in tests/commands/pr/test_list.py) - The two threads are duplicates of the same issue
2. **Thread 3** (line 133): Default parameter value - Classification: **Actionable** (code style violation) - Pre-existing: **false** (new code in this PR) - Complexity: **local** (single line parameter removal) - Author: `github-actions` bot
3. **Thread 4** (line 308): Temporary variable assignment simplification - Classification: **Actionable** (code style suggestion) - Pre-existing: **false** (modification in this PR) - Complexity: **local** (single line inlining) - Author: `github-actions` bot
4. **Thread 5** (line 435): Duplicate ternary assignment pattern - Classification: **Actionable** (code style suggestion) - Pre-existing: **false** (modification in this PR) - Complexity: **single_file** (related pattern at line 308 in same file) - Author: `github-actions` bot
**Discussion comments**: All discussion comments are informational (workflow status, CI reviews summary) - none are actionable requests.
**File restructuring**: No renames, splits, or moves detected (only additions in one file), so no pre-existing bot issues.
Here's the complete JSON output:

Consider extracting to a shared helper function" }, { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5zKBv_", "type": "review", "path": "src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py", "line": 130, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Add Layer 4 business logic tests for new --include-learn feature", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "original_comment": "New feature code added (`--include-learn` flag and filtering logic) without corresponding test file. Erk requires Layer 4 business logic tests for feature code that modifies filtering behavior. Consider adding test cases to tests/commands/pr/test_list.py covering both --include-learn and default exclude-learn behavior." } ], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 7, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0, 1] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [2] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [3, 4] } ], "error": null } ```
